meaning: adj.在那边的,较远的,将来的,隐蔽的
examples: Sheila had an ulterior motive for trying to help Stan.
I have an ulterior motive in offering to help you.
Relevant report is totally groundless and out of ulterior motives.
On the left, Mr. Mamet is accused of having ulterior motives for his political shift.
But some will inevitably suspect an ulterior motive, at least in the timing of the attack.
